title: Red Gravy

instruction.title: 1. Roast & Blend Tomatoes
steps: Roast tomatoes over an open flame or in the oven until lightly charred.
Allow to cool slightly.
Peel the tomatoes and blend into a smooth puree.

instruction.title: 2. Cook the Onions
steps: Heat oil or ghee in a heavy-bottom pan.
Add cumin seeds and allow them to sizzle.
Add chopped onions.
Cook on low to medium heat until deep golden brown.

instruction.title: 3. Build the Masala Base
steps: Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies.
Sauté for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, and coriander powder.
Roast the spices briefly without burning.

instruction.title: 4. Add Tomato Puree
steps: Pour in the tomato puree.
Cook on medium heat.
Stir occasionally until the mixture thickens.
Continue cooking until oil separates from the masala.

instruction.title: 5. Simmer & Finish
steps: Add water or stock to achieve a pourable consistency.
Simmer for 10–15 minutes.
Crush kasuri methi between your palms and add to the gravy.
Add garam masala.
Adjust salt and sugar to taste.

chefTips: Cook onions slowly and patiently for maximum flavor depth.
Replace part of the oil with ghee for a richer restaurant-style taste.
Keep the gravy slightly thick during storage; thin with stock or water when using.
A small pinch of sugar balances tomato acidity without making the gravy sweet.
Refrigerate for 3–4 days in an airtight container.
Freeze for up to 1 month in portion-sized containers or ice-cube trays.
Use as a base for paneer, chicken, vegetable, kofta, or curry dishes.
